hyperedges.label: Fireside Engineering Constraint Set (MSRV, boundaries, TEA, errors)
Four Traversal Operations (Next, Choose, Goto, Back)
Rust Reference Implementation Layering (core -> engine -> tui -> cli)

nodes.label: Fast Path Query on Existing Graph
nodes.rationale: If graphify-out/graph.json already exists and the request is a natural-language question, skip detection and rebuild entirely and run graphify query directly - the graph is already built, so re-extraction wastes tokens and time.
nodes.norm_label: fast path query on existing graph

nodes.label: AST Structural Extraction
nodes.rationale: Deterministic and free; runs in parallel with semantic extraction because the two operate on different file types, saving 5-15s on large corpora.

nodes.label: Semantic Extraction via Parallel Subagents
nodes.rationale: All chunk subagents must be dispatched in a single message with general-purpose type; sequential dispatch is 5-10x slower and read-only Explore agents silently drop chunk files.

nodes.label: Semantic Extraction Cache
nodes.rationale: Checking the per-file cache before dispatching subagents avoids re-paying LLM token cost for unchanged files.

nodes.label: Community Detection and Labeling
nodes.rationale: Plain-language community labels are written by the host LLM after clustering because labels affect suggested-question phrasing, so the report is regenerated once labels exist.

nodes.label: Graphify Honesty Rules
nodes.rationale: Never invent an edge (use AMBIGUOUS when unsure), always show raw token cost and cohesion scores - the graph's value depends on an honest audit trail.

nodes.label: Discrete Confidence Rubric
nodes.rationale: Models follow discrete rubrics better than continuous ranges; production showed a bimodal collapse (>50% at 0.5, >40% at 0.85+) when a range was offered, so confidence values are restricted to a fixed set and 0.5 is banned.

nodes.label: Deterministic Node ID Format
nodes.rationale: IDs must be deterministic from the label alone and match the AST extractor's parent-dir+stem scheme; chunk suffixes or path variants create orphan ghost-duplicate nodes.

nodes.label: Constrained Query Vocabulary Expansion
nodes.rationale: The query CLI matches via case-folded substring + IDF with no stemming, synonyms, or cross-language matching, so the question must be expanded against the graph's actual vocabulary - without inventing tokens - or the answer collapses to noise.

nodes.label: Save-Result Feedback Loop
nodes.rationale: Each query/path/explain answer is saved back into the graph so the next --update extracts the Q&A as a node, improving future queries.

nodes.label: Incremental Update Flow
nodes.rationale: Only re-extracts new or changed files; code-only changes skip semantic extraction entirely because AST needs no LLM.

nodes.label: Build-Merge with Prune
nodes.rationale: build_merge reads graph.json directly without a NetworkX round-trip so edge direction is preserved (#801), and old nodes for changed files are pruned before insertion to avoid collapsing same-named symbols across files (#1178).

nodes.label: Whisper Transcription with Domain Hint
nodes.rationale: The host LLM composes a one-sentence domain hint from god-node labels and passes it as Whisper's initial prompt - no separate API call needed - to improve transcription accuracy.

nodes.label: Folder Watcher Auto-Rebuild
nodes.rationale: Debounce (default 3s) waits until file activity stops so a wave of parallel agent writes does not trigger one rebuild per file; code changes rebuild via AST with no LLM, doc changes only set a needs_update flag.

nodes.label: GitHub Repo Clone Flow
nodes.rationale: Clones into ~/.graphify/repos/<owner>/<repo> and reuses existing clones; merged nodes carry a repo attribute for filtering by origin.

nodes.label: Cross-Repo Graph Merge
nodes.rationale: For monorepos the CLI is used per subfolder because the skill pipeline would clobber a shared graphify-out/; per-subfolder graphs are then merged at the root.

nodes.label: Post-Commit Auto-Rebuild Hook
nodes.rationale: Triggers once per commit with no background process; appends to an existing post-commit hook rather than replacing it.

nodes.label: Native CLAUDE.md Integration
nodes.rationale: graphify claude install writes a ## graphify section into CLAUDE.md so Claude checks the graph before answering codebase questions in every future session.

nodes.label: Token Reduction Benchmark
nodes.rationale: Only run when total_words > 5000 - for small corpora the graph's value is structural clarity, not token compression.

nodes.label: Claude Fable Planning Prompt
nodes.rationale: Treat docs and TypeSpec source as primary evidence, never memory; do not claim test success without running the command.

nodes.label: Protocol Spec Drift Audit
nodes.rationale: Gaps must be explained with evidence, the repo location reflecting the mismatch, and a concrete closing change - distinguishing spec gap vs implementation bug vs UX issue.

nodes.label: Token-Efficient Phased Roadmap
nodes.rationale: The plan must favor small well-bounded steps over rewrites so a smaller model such as Sonnet or MAI Code 1 Flash can execute it efficiently.

nodes.label: MSRV 1.88 Constraint
nodes.rationale: Workspace MSRV is 1.88 (resolver 3, 2024 edition); any dependency raising the MSRV requires an explicit user decision.

nodes.label: Fireside Crate Boundary Rules
nodes.rationale: fireside-core gets no I/O or UI deps, fireside-engine excludes ratatui/crossterm/clap, fireside-tui does no direct file I/O, fireside-cli does no rendering - keeping protocol types, business logic, and UI separable and testable.

nodes.label: TEA Single Mutation Invariant
nodes.rationale: App::update in fireside-tui is the only function that mutates App state, keeping the TUI predictable and patterns that move mutation elsewhere are rejected.

nodes.label: Error Handling Stratification
nodes.rationale: thiserror typed errors in each library crate (CoreError, EngineError, TuiError) with anyhow only at the CLI boundary; no anyhow inside libraries and no raw Box<dyn Error>.

nodes.label: Verify-Before-Advising Context7 Rule
nodes.rationale: Crate APIs must be verified with Context7 before recommending because outdated Rust APIs cause hard-to-debug compilation failures; never recommend from training memory alone.

nodes.label: Context7 Documentation Expert Agent
nodes.rationale: Documentation-first: resolve-library-id then get-library-docs before answering any library question, because training data may be outdated and hallucinated APIs destroy user trust.

nodes.label: Version Upgrade Checking Workflow
nodes.rationale: Always read the workspace dependency file, compare against the latest registry version, fetch docs for both versions, and provide migration guidance - hiding upgrade availability is forbidden.

nodes.label: Documentation Writer Agent
nodes.rationale: Writes for junior and mid-level engineers first; accuracy is non-negotiable and conflicting source material must be surfaced rather than silently resolved.

nodes.label: Documentation Writer Prompt Workflow
nodes.rationale: Clarify document type, audience, scope, and source of truth, then propose an outline and await approval before drafting - preventing wasted full drafts on misunderstood requests.

nodes.label: Diataxis Framework
nodes.rationale: Tutorials (lesson), how-to guides (recipe), reference (dictionary), and explanation (discussion) have distinct purposes that must not be mixed in one document.

nodes.label: Rust Best Practices Instructions
nodes.rationale: Prefers readability, explicit types, and incremental verifiable changes; logic stays in fireside-engine, protocol types in fireside-core, UI in fireside-tui, and changes must be small enough for a token-efficient model to implement safely.

nodes.label: No-Heredoc File Operations Rule
nodes.rationale: Terminal heredoc operations are broken in VS Code's Copilot integration - tab completion, quote escaping failures, and exit-code-130 truncation corrupt files - so all file content must be written via file editing tools, never shell redirection.

nodes.label: ADR Skill
nodes.rationale: ADRs are written only for significant architectural decisions (crate swaps, protocol-breaking changes, cross-cutting conventions), not routine fixes; every decision must list honest trade-offs.

nodes.label: Nygard ADR Format
nodes.rationale: Status/Context/Decision/Consequences structure with sequential zero-padded numbering filed under docs/src/content/docs/explanation/ and registered in the Astro sidebar.

nodes.label: PRD Skill
nodes.rationale: Requires a discovery interview (at least 2 clarifying questions) before drafting and concrete measurable criteria instead of vague adjectives like fast or intuitive.

nodes.label: Refactor Skill
nodes.rationale: Behavior-preserving, small-step refactoring with tests as a precondition; without tests you are editing, not refactoring.

nodes.label: Rust CI Workflow
nodes.rationale: Lint (fmt, clippy -D warnings, doc -D warnings), nextest on Ubuntu and macOS, and a dedicated MSRV check gate every crates/ change.

nodes.label: Security Audit Workflow
nodes.rationale: cargo audit and cargo deny run on Cargo.lock/deny.toml changes plus a weekly Monday schedule, catching advisories even when the repo is idle.

nodes.label: tsp-output Commit Verification
nodes.rationale: CI fails if compiling protocol/main.tsp produces schemas that differ from the committed tsp-output/, preventing silent drift between TypeSpec source and generated JSON Schema.

nodes.label: Fireside Protocol
nodes.rationale: Designed for branching, non-linear content as a directed graph of nodes; chooses explicit edges (no implicit sequential fallback) so every traversal edge is visible in the document, and stays implementation-agnostic so the protocol defines behavior rather than rendering.

nodes.label: TypeSpec Domain Model (source of truth)
nodes.rationale: TypeSpec is the design surface and the generated JSON Schema 2020-12 files are the machine-readable validation surface; any runtime that parses JSON and keeps a cursor plus a history stack can conform.

nodes.label: fireside-cli crate
nodes.rationale: Deliberately thin binary entry point: parse args with clap, bootstrap/tear down the terminal, dispatch to command handlers; the interesting logic lives in the engine and TUI crates.

nodes.label: Terminal Lifecycle Pattern
nodes.rationale: The restore block (disable raw mode, leave alternate screen) runs unconditionally before result propagation so the user's terminal is never left in raw mode even on panic or error; isolating run_event_loop from lifecycle setup makes it testable without a real terminal.

nodes.label: CLI Event Loop
nodes.rationale: Polls with a timeout so the application can tick animations (transitions, elapsed timer) even when the user is idle; follows the TEA discipline: draw, poll, translate raw event to Action, update.

nodes.label: fireside-core crate
nodes.rationale: Owns every type appearing in a Fireside JSON document and nothing more: no rendering, no I/O, no validation beyond serde, keeping the serialization layer thin and honest.

nodes.label: Graph (runtime repr)
nodes.rationale: Adds a node_index HashMap for O(1) lookup by ID; the only construction path is Graph::from_file, so the index can never be inconsistent with the nodes vec.

nodes.label: GraphFile (wire repr)
nodes.rationale: Matches the JSON schema field-for-field; the wire/runtime boundary keeps the node_index optimization out of the schema where it would be wrong to encode.

nodes.label: Node (core type)
nodes.rationale: Helper methods next_override, branch_point, after_target are the contract the engine uses for traversal decisions, avoiding Option-chaining at call sites.

nodes.label: ContentBlock enum (8 variants)
nodes.rationale: Serde internally-tagged union with kind as discriminator and kebab-case renames, producing a clean human-readable wire format with no wrapper key.

nodes.label: ListItem custom deserialization
nodes.rationale: Accepts both a bare string and a { text, children } struct so simple lists stay terse while nesting remains possible.

nodes.label: fireside-engine crate
nodes.rationale: Knows what Fireside does but nothing about how it looks: no ratatui or crossterm imports, so it is testable in isolation and could power a web renderer or headless validator. Error handling is stratified: typed EngineError for library functions, anyhow at the application boundary.

nodes.label: PresentationSession
nodes.rationale: Simple value carrier with all-pub fields; correctness is maintained by engine functions keeping graph, traversal, dirty flag, and command history in sync rather than by access control.

nodes.label: TraversalEngine
nodes.rationale: Small state machine with private current index and history stack; the next priority chain (traversal.next override, then traversal.after rejoin, then sequential advance) means a branch looks like any other set of nodes at the encoding level.

nodes.label: Command / CommandHistory (undo-redo)
nodes.rationale: Every command has a computable inverse stored alongside it as a HistoryEntry, so undo/redo needs no diff logic — the inverse is always a concrete Command that exactly reverses the original.

nodes.label: validate_graph
nodes.rationale: Returns Vec<Diagnostic> rather than a single error so tools can report all issues at once; an empty vec means structurally valid.

nodes.label: Loader (load_graph / save_graph)
nodes.rationale: Returns anyhow::Result so user-facing errors carry full context; save_graph reconstructs a GraphFile from the runtime Graph so output is always clean wire format; load_graph_from_str skips file I/O for tests.

nodes.label: TEA (The Elm Architecture) Discipline
nodes.rationale: App::update is the only function that mutates App state; render functions take immutable references and produce widgets, making the render path trivially testable and letting you reason about state by reading update alone.

nodes.label: Action enum (~35 variants)
nodes.rationale: Actions are the vocabulary of intent: every user interaction becomes exactly one Action before update, so keybindings, mouse events, and programmatic triggers all speak the same language.

nodes.label: Theme System
nodes.rationale: Default theme uses Color::Reset so it adapts to the terminal's own palette; ThemeFile holds Option<String> per field so partial theme files merge with defaults.

nodes.label: TUI Rendering Pipeline
nodes.rationale: All render functions in ui/ and render/ are pure: they take &App plus a Frame and never mutate state, making snapshot-style testing tractable.

nodes.label: iTerm2 Theme Import
nodes.rationale: Converts .itermcolors XML plists to ThemeFile JSON so any iTerm2 color scheme can be reused without manual color transcription.

nodes.label: Getting Started Guide (First Fireside Graph)
nodes.rationale: Deliberately the smallest graph that still demonstrates entry, branching, rejoin, and a terminal node — one role per node so the protocol's core ideas show without extra branches.

nodes.label: Domain Vocabulary
nodes.rationale: Separates canonical protocol terms (Graph, Node, BranchPoint) from conversational aliases (Session, Moment, Question) used only in onboarding; canonical terms are mandatory in protocol text, schemas, and engine APIs.

nodes.label: Engine Conformance Contract (0.1.0)
nodes.rationale: Small but strict: parse and validate documents, implement Next/Choose/Goto/Back, render all seven core block kinds, preserve traversal and history rules — a minimal mandatory core so engines agree on a shared baseline.

nodes.label: Explicit Edges Over Implicit Sequence
nodes.rationale: Traversal targets are declared, not inferred from array order; this keeps navigation explicit and stable and lets a document move between runtimes without changing meaning (portability first).

nodes.label: Graph, Not Deck
nodes.rationale: If PowerPoint is a stack of slides, Fireside is a graph of nodes with explicit exits; node order in the file is document organization, not presentation order. Authors think in nodes, presenters think in moves (next/choose/goto/back), engine authors think in state (current ID, history stack, lookup table).

nodes.label: Graph (protocol type)
nodes.rationale: nodes is required with minItems 1; the first node is the graph entry point; defaults provide graph-wide view-mode and transition fallbacks.

nodes.label: Node (protocol type)
nodes.rationale: view-mode and transition resolve node-level value first, then graph defaults, then built-in default; traversal may be a string shorthand, an object, or absent (terminal).

nodes.label: ContainerBlock
nodes.rationale: The only core block that nests other blocks; layout is a local arrangement hint (stack, columns, center), not a global theme instruction.

nodes.label: Traversal (protocol type)
nodes.rationale: Object form MUST NOT contain both next and branch-point; string shorthand remains valid for the simple single-edge case.

nodes.label: NodeId Scalar
nodes.rationale: Non-empty string; MUST be unique within a graph and SHOULD use kebab-case.

nodes.label: Goto Operation
nodes.rationale: Because Goto is an explicit presenter command, it bypasses branch-point gating.

nodes.label: Back Operation
nodes.rationale: Pops one history entry and MUST NOT push during the same operation, so Back reflects the presenter's actual path rather than a recomputed approximation.

nodes.label: Branch-Point Gating
nodes.rationale: If a node has a branch point, Next is blocked and the engine MUST wait for Choose — branching is a real decision, not a default path that can be skipped past.

nodes.label: History Invariants
nodes.rationale: Choose/Goto/moving Next push exactly one entry, Back pops one and pushes none, failed operations never mutate history, and entries are node IDs not array indices — these invariants keep traversal understandable after branching and rejoining.

nodes.label: Graph Patterns (Linear, Branch-Rejoin, Hub-Spoke, Open World)
nodes.rationale: All patterns are valid compositions of the same core traversal rules; each branch endpoint wires its own explicit return target, which keeps nested branches, shared nodes, and cycles explicit.
nodes.norm_label: graph patterns (linear, branch-rejoin, hub-spoke, open world)

nodes.label: Kebab-Case Wire Naming
nodes.rationale: Core property names and enum values use kebab-case on the wire (speaker-notes, branch-point, view-mode: fullscreen); documents are UTF-8 JSON, .fireside.json is the preferred extension but engines MUST NOT require a specific extension.

nodes.label: Two-Layer Validation (Schema + Semantic)
nodes.rationale: Schema validation checks shape against Graph.json; semantic checks catch graph-specific errors (unique node IDs, resolvable traversal targets, non-empty branch options, next/branch-point exclusivity) because a document can be structurally valid JSON and still unusable as a graph.

nodes.label: Core Runtime Guarantees
nodes.rationale: Non-normative but recommended: graph data immutable after load, state mutation in one update path, history as a strict LIFO stack, deterministic rendering for equivalent state; keep protocol state, view state, input mapping, and rendering hints separate.

nodes.label: TypeSpec Emitter Config (tspconfig.yaml)
nodes.rationale: Emits @typespec/json-schema to {output-dir}/schemas with int64-strategy string; linter extends @fireside/typespec/recommended.

links.source_location: once graph.json exists the fast path takes over

hyperedges.label: Fireside Engineering Constraint Set (MSRV, boundaries, TEA, errors)
Four Traversal Operations (Next, Choose, Goto, Back)
Rust Reference Implementation Layering (core -> engine -> tui -> cli)
